scenario: Next.js SSR render path tracing and request ID threading

steps.step: Orient in the Next.js server architecture

map_quality.rationale: Provides file:line for BaseServer (base-server.ts:177), NextNodeServer (next-server.ts:175), DevServer, NextServer wrapper, plus the three major subdirectories (app-render, route-modules, response-cache) with concrete files. The agent could navigate directly to extend any server class.

specificity.rationale: Class names are exact (BaseServer, NextNodeServer, AppPageRouteModule). Pages-vs-App split is made concrete with render.tsx:1622 vs app-render/app-render.tsx:3077, lazyRenderPagesPage vs lazyRenderAppPage, with exact line numbers.

justification.rationale: Subdirectories are annotated with brief purpose ('App Router rendering', 'per-route-kind dispatch', 'Node ResponseCache class'), and the shared-entry-then-branch on components.isAppPath is explained. However, the WHY of the class hierarchy (Edge vs Node base, custom-server seam rationale) is not really articulated — DevServer and NextServer wrapper are listed without explaining why they exist.

uncertainty.rationale: Mentions DevServer (next-dev-server.ts:122) and the response-cache web.ts edge variant, hinting at dev/prod and edge splits. But does not flag Turbopack vs webpack, or call out which code path actually runs in each configuration branch as a caveat the agent should verify.

map_quality.rationale: The 8-step pipeline walk names router-server.ts:226 requestHandlerImpl, base-server.ts:877 handleRequest, render:1852, renderToResponseImpl:2579, findPageComponents → loadComponents:355, renderHTML:604, then both render.tsx:1622/457 and app-render.tsx:3077/2568 — exactly the layers an agent needs to insert a hook with file:line precision.

specificity.rationale: Distinguishes wrappers from Impls throughout (renderToHTML vs renderToHTMLImpl, renderToHTMLOrFlight vs renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl, handleRequest vs handleRequestImpl), names router-server as the outermost entry, and cites lazyRenderAppPage / lazyRenderPagesPage as the dispatch seam.

justification.rationale: Explains the App-vs-Pages branch at base-server.ts:2043 isAppPath, what loadComponentsImpl resolves (manifest, ComponentMod, font/loadable manifests), and why workAsyncStorage.run wraps the Impl. Could say a bit more about why findPageComponents lives at the boundary, but the rationale is largely present.

uncertainty.rationale: Mentions response-cache via route-module.ts:1130 and notes RSC/prefetch metadata and Flight streaming in renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl, but doesn't explicitly flag dynamic vs static/PPR diversions, dev-vs-prod, or edge-runtime variants that could change which path runs.

map_quality.rationale: Lists three callers with file:line and enclosing function (AppPageRouteModule.render at module.ts:160, lazyRenderAppPage at module.render.ts:9, NextNodeServer.renderHTMLImpl at next-server.ts:635), names the definition site at app-render.tsx:3077, and identifies re-export sites including module.ts:210 and module.compiled. Agent could confidently change the signature.

specificity.rationale: Callers cited with file:line + enclosing fn, distinguishes import site (:9) vs call site (:160), names the re-export at module.ts:210 and the generated module.compiled. Type signature site (AppPageRender) also named.

justification.rationale: Each caller annotated with its role (class method, lazy wrapper forwarding args, renderHTMLImpl dispatch via lazyRenderAppPage), and the module.compiled indirection is explained as runtime re-export. Could go slightly deeper on why three layers exist.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the module.compiled generation/build-time indirection as a dynamic dispatch concern, but doesn't explicitly call out edge-runtime variants or whether route-module wiring registers additional callers at runtime that static lookup might miss.

steps.step: Assess where to thread a request ID

map_quality.rationale: Names RequestMeta (request-meta.ts:51), handleRequest (base-server.ts:877), response-cache interaction (route-module.ts:1130, response-cache/index.ts:107), and provides a complete edit_map with file:line per site. Agent could open a PR directly from this.

specificity.rationale: Concrete infra cites: RequestMeta interface line, getRequestMeta:348, INTERNAL_HEADERS in server-ipc/utils.ts, createWorkStore at app-render.tsx:3145. Tests are named at directory/glob level rather than exact files, and the NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER constant location is proposed rather than confirmed.

justification.rationale: Explicitly explains the RequestMeta-vs-ALS tradeoff ('ALS is only entered inside renderToHTMLOrFlight at app-render.tsx:3155; not active in router-server / base-server / load-components phases') and the cache-key implication ('baking x-request-id into a cached entry would leak the first requester's id').

uncertainty.rationale: Flags response-cache key/body leakage, edge variant via response-cache/web.ts implicit, header trust/forgery boundary citing CLAUDE.md, send-payload header clobbering, DevServer path, and custom-server wrapper. Middleware interaction is only lightly touched.
